 {"id":"bd-1323","title":"E2E Tests: Dashboard visual regression with VHS recordings","description":"## Overview
End-to-end visual regression tests using VHS (terminal recording) to verify the dashboard renders correctly across different scenarios.

## Prerequisites
- VHS installed: \`brew install vhs\` or \`go install github.com/charmbracelet/vhs@latest\`
- ffmpeg for video processing
- ImageMagick for screenshot comparison

## Test File Location
- tests/e2e/dashboard_visual_test.go
- testdata/vhs/*.tape (VHS tape files)
- testdata/golden/*.png (golden screenshots)

## VHS Tape Files

### 1. Basic Render Test
\`\`\`tape
# File: testdata/vhs/dashboard-basic.tape
Output testdata/vhs/dashboard-basic.gif
Set FontSize 14
Set Width 1200
Set Height 800
Set Theme "Catppuccin Mocha"
Set TypingSpeed 50ms

# Start dashboard with test session
Type "ntm dashboard test-session"
Enter
Sleep 3s

# Take screenshot after initial render
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/basic-initial.png

# Verify no duplication by checking status bar area
Sleep 1s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/basic-stable.png
\`\`\`

### 2. Resize Test
\`\`\`tape
# File: testdata/vhs/dashboard-resize.tape
Output testdata/vhs/dashboard-resize.gif
Set FontSize 14
Set Width 1200
Set Height 800
Set Theme "Catppuccin Mocha"

Type "ntm dashboard test-session"
Enter
Sleep 2s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/resize-wide.png

# Narrow terminal
Set Width 800
Sleep 1s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/resize-narrow.png

# Very narrow
Set Width 600
Sleep 1s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/resize-compact.png

# Back to wide
Set Width 1600
Sleep 1s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/resize-ultrawide.png
\`\`\`

### 3. Navigation Test
\`\`\`tape
# File: testdata/vhs/dashboard-navigation.tape
Output testdata/vhs/dashboard-navigation.gif
Set FontSize 14
Set Width 1200
Set Height 800

Type "ntm dashboard test-session"
Enter
Sleep 2s

# Navigate down
Type "j"
Sleep 300ms
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/nav-down1.png

Type "j"
Sleep 300ms
Type "j"
Sleep 300ms
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/nav-down3.png

# Navigate up
Type "k"
Sleep 300ms
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/nav-up.png

# Select by number
Type "5"
Sleep 500ms
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/nav-select5.png
\`\`\`

### 4. Refresh Test (No Accumulation)
\`\`\`tape
# File: testdata/vhs/dashboard-refresh.tape
Output testdata/vhs/dashboard-refresh.gif
Set FontSize 14
Set Width 1200
Set Height 800

Type "ntm dashboard test-session"
Enter
Sleep 2s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/refresh-before.png

# Manual refresh
Type "r"
Sleep 2s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/refresh-after1.png

Type "r"
Sleep 2s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/refresh-after2.png

Type "r"
Sleep 2s
Screenshot testdata/screenshots/refresh-after3.png
\`\`\`

## Visual Comparison Script
\`\`\`bash
#!/bin/bash
# scripts/visual-regression.sh

set -euo pipefail

TESTDATA="testdata"
GOLDEN="$TESTDATA/golden"
SCREENSHOTS="$TESTDATA/screenshots"
THRESHOLD=100  # Maximum pixel difference allowed

echo "Running VHS tapes..."
for tape in $TESTDATA/vhs/*.tape; do
    echo "  Running: $tape"
    vhs "$tape" 2>&1 | tee -a vhs.log
done

echo "Comparing screenshots..."
FAILED=0
for golden in $GOLDEN/*.png; do
    name=$(basename "$golden")
    screenshot="$SCREENSHOTS/$name"
    
    if [[ ! -f "$screenshot" ]]; then
        echo "  MISSING: $name"
        FAILED=1
        continue
    fi
    
    # Compare with ImageMagick
    diff=$(compare -metric AE "$golden" "$screenshot" /dev/null 2>&1 || true)
    
    if [[ "$diff" -gt "$THRESHOLD" ]]; then
        echo "  FAIL: $name (diff: $diff pixels)"
        # Generate diff image for debugging
        compare "$golden" "$screenshot" "$TESTDATA/diff-$name" || true
        FAILED=1
    else
        echo "  PASS: $name (diff: $diff pixels)"
    fi
done

if [[ "$FAILED" -eq 1 ]]; then
    echo ""
    echo "Visual regression tests FAILED"
    echo "Check testdata/diff-*.png for differences"
    exit 1
fi

echo ""
echo "All visual regression tests PASSED"
\`\`\`

## E2E Test Wrapper
\`\`\`go
// tests/e2e/dashboard_visual_test.go
func TestDashboard_VisualRegression(t *testing.T) {
    if os.Getenv("RUN_VISUAL_TESTS") == "" {
        t.Skip("set RUN_VISUAL_TESTS=1 to run visual regression tests")
    }
    
    // Run visual regression script
    cmd := exec.Command("bash", "scripts/visual-regression.sh")
    cmd.Dir = projectRoot()
    output, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
    
    t.Log(string(output))
    
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("visual regression failed: %v", err)
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Specific Assertions

### No Status Bar Duplication
Check that screenshots contain exactly one instance of status bar text.

### Proper Layout at All Sizes
- 80x24: Compact mode, essential elements visible
- 120x40: Standard mode, all panels visible
- 200x60: Wide mode, expanded layout

### Animation Stability
Multiple screenshots at same state should be visually identical (except for animations).

## CI Integration
\`\`\`yaml
visual-tests:
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - name: Install VHS
      run: |
        brew install vhs imagemagick ffmpeg
    - name: Run visual tests
      run: |
        RUN_VISUAL_TESTS=1 go test -v ./tests/e2e/...
    - name: Upload diff images
      if: failure()
      uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
      with:
        name: visual-diffs
        path: testdata/diff-*.png
\`\`\`

## Updating Golden Files
\`\`\`bash
# After intentional UI changes, update golden files:
UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 RUN_VISUAL_TESTS=1 go test -v ./tests/e2e/...

# Or manually:
cp testdata/screenshots/*.png testdata/golden/
\`\`\`

## Logging
All test runs should log:
- VHS version
- Terminal size for each tape
- Screenshot file paths
- Pixel difference for each comparison
- Full command output on failure","notes":"VHS tape files created in testdata/vhs/. E2E test wrapper in tests/e2e/dashboard_visual_test.go. Visual regression script at scripts/visual-regression.sh. Directories testdata/golden and testdata/screenshots created. Golden images need to be generated when VHS is installed. Infrastructure is complete, ready to generate golden images with: UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 go test -v -run TestUpdateGoldenImages ./tests/e2e","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:13:09.085341313Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T06:15:57.067104553Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T06:15:57.067005807Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1323","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:24Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1323","depends_on_id":"bd-3b3f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:24Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1370q","title":"EPIC: Support Bundle (Diagnostics + Safe Sharing)","description":"## Summary
Add a first-class way to generate **safe, shareable diagnostics** for NTM issues.

Today users often paste logs/pane output manually (high leak risk, low reproducibility). A support bundle command can capture:
- environment + tool versions
- effective config
- recent event log slice
- relevant session snapshot(s)
- optional pane excerpts
…while applying redaction and respecting privacy mode.

## Goals
- One command produces a single archive file (`.zip` or `.tar.gz`).
- Safe-by-default: redaction enabled by default; explicit overrides required.
- Deterministic manifest with checksums for integrity.

## Non-goals
- Uploading bundles to third-party services (keep local; users can share manually).

## Success Criteria
- Bundle generation works without running sessions (still includes env/config).
- Works in robot mode with stable JSON output.
- Includes enough info to reproduce common bugs (tmux base-index, config parsing, etc.).
- Comprehensive unit + E2E tests with detailed logs.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:26:55.667286751Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:26:55.667286751Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-13rct","title":"Task: Implement EarlyStopDetector for marginal utility stopping","description":"# Task: EarlyStopDetector Implementation

Stop ensemble when new findings rate drops below threshold.

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/early_stop.go
type EarlyStopConfig struct {
    MinAgentsBeforeStop int     // Run at least N agents first
    FindingsThreshold   float64 // Stop if findings/token < threshold
    SimilarityThreshold float64 // Stop if output similarity > threshold
    WindowSize          int     // Evaluate over last N agents
    Enabled             bool
}

type EarlyStopDetector struct {
    Config        EarlyStopConfig
    Outputs       []ModeOutput
    TokensSpent   []int
}

type StopDecision struct {
    ShouldStop    bool
    Reason        string
    FindingsRate  float64
    SimilarityScore float64
    AgentsRun     int
}

func NewEarlyStopDetector(cfg EarlyStopConfig) *EarlyStopDetector
func (d *EarlyStopDetector) RecordOutput(output ModeOutput, tokens int)
func (d *EarlyStopDetector) ShouldStop() StopDecision
func (d *EarlyStopDetector) CalculateFindingsRate() float64
func (d *EarlyStopDetector) CalculateSimilarity() float64
```

## Stopping Criteria

### Findings Rate Drop
```
Rate = (new unique findings in window) / (tokens spent in window)
Stop if: rate < threshold AND agents_run >= min_agents
```

### Output Similarity
```
Similarity = average pairwise similarity of outputs in window
Stop if: similarity > threshold (outputs are redundant)
```

## Logging
- Log each decision with metrics
- Log when approaching threshold
- Log final stop reason

 {"id":"bd-144k","title":"Fix help/keybinding component to render hints once","description":"## Problem
Keyboard navigation hints ("↑↓ navigate", "1-9 select", "z zoom", etc.) appear multiple times in the dashboard footer area due to content overflow.

## Screenshot Evidence
Multiple lines showing: "ti navigate 1-9 select z zoom r refresh ? help q quit"

## Code Location
- internal/tui/dashboard/dashboard.go:2084 - renderHelpBar() called
- internal/tui/components/help.go:63-110 - RenderHelpBar implementation

## Analysis
The RenderHelpBar function itself is correctly implemented:
- Uses lipgloss.Width() for width calculations (line 93-101)
- Returns a fresh string each call
- No state accumulation

The duplication occurs because:
1. Help bar is appended to main content without height management
2. When content overflows terminal, old frames with help bars remain visible
3. Same root cause as status bar duplication (bd-2usu)

## Fix Required
This is fixed by implementing proper footer region management (see bd-2usu). The help bar should be:
1. Part of the fixed footer region
2. Rendered after the content area is height-constrained
3. Not included in any scrollable viewport

## Additional Improvements
1. Ensure help hints are context-aware (show relevant hints for focused panel)
2. Add width-gated progressive disclosure (fewer hints on narrow terminals)
3. Consider sticky help bar that always shows essential hints

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Help bar appears exactly once at the bottom
- [ ] Help hints update based on focused panel
- [ ] Narrow terminals show abbreviated hints
- [ ] No duplication on resize or scroll

## Unit Tests
1. TestHelpBar_RendersOnce - "navigate" appears exactly once in View()
2. TestHelpBar_ContextualHints - hints change based on focusedPanel
3. TestHelpBar_WidthTruncation - narrow width removes rightmost hints
4. TestHelpBar_NoAccumulation - multiple View() calls produce same output

## Logging
- DEBUG: Log number of hints rendered
- DEBUG: Log width truncation decisions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:13:39.445792941Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T09:08:25.382075262Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:08:25.381982357Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-144k","depends_on_id":"bd-1xoe","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:24Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-144k","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:24Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-14h3d","title":"Task: Implement FindingsVelocity metric","description":"# Task: FindingsVelocity Implementation

Track insights per token spent for efficiency analysis.

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/velocity.go
type VelocityTracker struct {
    Entries []VelocityEntry
}

type VelocityEntry struct {
    ModeID        string
    ModeName      string
    TokensSpent   int
    FindingsCount int
    UniqueFindings int
    Velocity      float64 // findings per 1k tokens
}

type VelocityReport struct {
    Overall        float64 // total unique findings / total tokens * 1000
    PerMode        []VelocityEntry
    HighPerformers []string // modes above average
    LowPerformers  []string // modes below threshold
    Suggestions    []string
}

func NewVelocityTracker() *VelocityTracker
func (v *VelocityTracker) RecordOutput(modeID string, output ModeOutput, tokens int)
func (v *VelocityTracker) CalculateVelocity() *VelocityReport
func (v *VelocityTracker) Render() string
```

## Velocity Calculation
```
velocity = (unique_findings / tokens_spent) * 1000

Unique findings are de-duplicated by:
- normalized finding text (lowercase + trim)
- evidence_pointer when present
```

## Inputs
- Token counts from BudgetTracker / TokenEstimator (per-mode)
- Outputs from normalized ModeOutput

## Edge Cases
- tokens_spent == 0 → skip or mark velocity as 0 with warning
- empty findings → velocity 0

## Visualization
```
Findings Velocity:
Overall: 2.3 findings / 1k tokens

Per Mode:
F1 Argument Mapping:    3.1 findings/1k (HIGH)
E2 Root Cause:          2.8 findings/1k
K1 Systems Thinking:    1.2 findings/1k (LOW)
L3 Meta-cognitive:      2.5 findings/1k
H2 Strategic:           1.9 findings/1k

Suggestion: K1 underperforming, consider early stop
```

## Logging Requirements
- Log tokens + findings per mode
- Log threshold crossings (below min velocity)

## Tests
- Unit: unique finding de-duplication
- Unit: zero-token handling
- Unit: per-mode and overall velocity math

 {"id":"bd-1zrjo","title":"TOON: Implement serialization wrapper supporting JSON and TOON","description":"Create abstraction layer for robot mode output serialization.

## Requirements

1. Single interface for serialization
2. Support JSON and TOON backends
3. Runtime switchable (per-command or global)
4. Handle errors gracefully (fallback to JSON)

## Implementation

```go
package serialize

type Format string

const (
    FormatJSON Format = "json"
    FormatTOON Format = "toon"
)

type Serializer interface {
    Serialize(v any) ([]byte, error)
    ContentType() string
}

func NewSerializer(format Format) Serializer {
    switch format {
    case FormatTOON:
        return &TOONSerializer{}
    default:
        return &JSONSerializer{}
    }
}
```

## Code Location
- internal/serialize/serialize.go
- internal/serialize/json.go
- internal/serialize/toon.go

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Serializer interface defined with Serialize() and ContentType() methods
2. JSONSerializer implements interface using encoding/json
3. TOONSerializer implements interface calling toon binary
4. NewSerializer factory function returns correct implementation
5. Fallback to JSON when toon binary not found
6. Error wrapping provides clear context

## Unit Tests (80% coverage target)
- internal/serialize/serialize_test.go
- Test round-trip JSON encoding/decoding
- Test TOON encoding with mock binary
- Test fallback behavior when toon not available

## E2E Test Script
```bash
#\!/bin/bash
# [E2E-SERIALIZER] Serialization Wrapper Tests
LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-SERIALIZER]"
echo "$LOG_PREFIX Testing serialization wrapper"
# Test JSON serialization works
TEST_DATA='{"key":"value"}'
echo "$TEST_DATA" | go run ./cmd/serialize-test/ --format json | jq -e '.' || exit 1
echo "$LOG_PREFIX JSON serialization passed"
```","acceptance_criteria":"1. Serialization wrapper supports JSON and TOON
2. Same Go types work with both formats
3. TOON output 40-60 percent smaller than JSON
4. Round-trip parsing verified
5. Unit tests for both formats
6. Benchmark comparing sizes","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-21T07:03:31.235798416Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T18:13:18.555102773Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1zrjo","depends_on_id":"bd-1u462","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:26Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zrjo","depends_on_id":"bd-6wc0a","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:26Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zrjo","depends_on_id":"bd-77bxi","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:26Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1zs9y","title":"Feature: Token Budget Advisor","description":"## Summary
Estimate token usage for selected modes and provide budget-aware recommendations before running an ensemble.

## Rationale
Token costs can be significant; users need visibility and alternatives.

## Implementation
- Per-mode estimation using token heuristics (bd-2t4cs) + historical calibration.
- `ntm ensemble estimate` shows per-mode breakdown + total estimate.
- `--budget` flag warns and suggests cheaper alternatives.
- Track actual vs. estimated to improve calibration over time.

## Logging
- Log estimates, calibration deltas, and budget warnings.

## Tests
- Unit: estimation accuracy + calibration adjustment
- Integration: budget warnings with sample configs
- E2E: estimate output + budget warning path

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Reasonably accurate estimates
- [ ] Per-mode breakdown available
- [ ] Budget warnings fire at thresholds
- [ ] Alternative mode suggestions
- [ ] Calibration improves over time

## Dependencies
- Budget control + tracking (bd-1dt84, bd-3d4tl)
- Token estimation heuristics (bd-2t4cs)
- Mode catalog (bd-11mtt)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:09:08.646964343Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T17:47:34.424225995Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1zs9y","depends_on_id":"bd-11mtt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:26Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zs9y","depends_on_id":"bd-1dt84","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:26Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zs9y","depends_on_id":"bd-1h4ox","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:26Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zs9y","depends_on_id":"bd-2t4cs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:26Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1zs9y","depends_on_id":"bd-3d4tl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:26Z","created_by":"import"}]}
+{"id":"bd-200tz","title":"Task: Unit tests for redaction-aware notifications","description":"## Scope

 {"id":"bd-209g","title":"Add Agent Mail integration - auto-register agents with MCP Agent Mail on spawn","description":"## Overview
Auto-register spawned agents with MCP Agent Mail for coordination.

## Implementation
When agents spawn, NTM:
1. Calls `mcp__mcp-agent-mail__ensure_project` with workspace path
2. For each agent, calls `mcp__mcp-agent-mail__register_agent` with:
   - program: 'claude-code' | 'codex-cli' | 'gemini-cli'
   - model: agent model
   - task_description: from spawn config or 'General development'

## Agent Names
Uses adjective+noun format per Agent Mail rules (e.g., 'GreenCastle', 'BlueLake').
Maps pane -> agent name for later reference.

## Error Handling
```go
type AgentMailError struct {
    Phase    string  // "ensure_project", "register_agent", "health_check"
    AgentIdx int     // Which agent failed (-1 for project-level)
    Cause    error
}
```

### Scenarios
1. **Agent Mail server not running**
   - Detect via health_check timeout (2s)
   - Log warning: "Agent Mail unavailable, skipping registration"
   - Continue spawn without registration
   - Set session flag: `agent_mail_available = false`

2. **ensure_project fails**
   - Log warning with error details
   - Continue spawn without registration
   - Retry on next operation

3. **register_agent fails for some agents**
   - Log warning for each failed agent
   - Continue with other agents
   - Return partial success in output

4. **Name collision**
   - Agent Mail auto-generates unique name
   - Store whatever name is returned

## Fallback Mode
When Agent Mail unavailable:
- File reservations disabled (warn user)
- Agent coordination disabled
- ntm assign still works (just no Agent Mail features)

## Output
```json
{
  "agent_mail": {
    "available": true,
    "project_registered": true,
    "agents_registered": 4,
    "agents_failed": 0,
    "agent_map": {
      "2": "GreenCastle",
      "3": "BlueLake",
      "4": "RedStone",
      "5": "PurpleBear"
    }
  }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Registers all agents on spawn
- [ ] Uses correct adjective+noun names
- [ ] Maps pane numbers to agent names
- [ ] Handles server unavailable gracefully (warning, continue)
- [ ] Handles partial registration failures
- [ ] Stores agent names in session state
- [ ] Falls back gracefully when unavailable
- [ ] Clear logging for all error scenarios

## Tests Required
- Unit test: Agent name generation
- Unit test: Pane-to-name mapping
- Unit test: Error handling for each scenario
- E2E test: Registration with real Agent Mail
- E2E test: Graceful degradation when unavailable","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:36:38.950986689Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T22:58:35.143387294Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T22:58:35.143294539Z","close_reason":"Implemented Agent Mail auto-registration on spawn. Added registerSpawnedAgents function in spawn.go that: (1) Ensures project exists via EnsureProject, (2) Creates unique agent identities for each spawned agent using CreateAgentIdentity, (3) Maps pane index to agent name, (4) Returns status in SpawnResponse JSON with available, project_registered, agents_registered, agents_failed, and agent_map fields. Also fixed pre-existing bugs: orphaned code in FormatRecoveryPrompt and missing UsagePercent field access in rotation.go.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-209g","depends_on_id":"bd-pjjk7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:26Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-t5ap9","title":"Task: Implement 'ntm ensemble modes' command for browsing mode catalog","description":"# Task: Implement 'ntm ensemble modes' Command

## Location
internal/cli/ensemble_modes.go

## Purpose
Allow users to browse and inspect available reasoning modes.

## Command Specification
```bash
ntm ensemble modes [command]

Commands:
  list    List all available modes
  show    Show detailed information about a mode

# List all modes
ntm ensemble modes list [flags]
  --category string   Filter by category (e.g., 'Causal', 'Ampliative')
  --json              Output as JSON

# Show mode details
ntm ensemble modes show <mode-id> [flags]
  --json              Output as JSON
```

## Implementation
```go
func newEnsembleModesCmd() *cobra.Command {
    cmd := &cobra.Command{
        Use:   'modes',
        Short: 'Browse reasoning modes',
    }
    
    cmd.AddCommand(newEnsembleModesListCmd())
    cmd.AddCommand(newEnsembleModesShowCmd())
    
    return cmd
}

func newEnsembleModesListCmd() *cobra.Command {
    var category string
    var jsonOutput bool
    
    return &cobra.Command{
        Use:   'list',
        Short: 'List all available reasoning modes',
        RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
            modes := catalog.ListModes()
            if category != '' {
                modes = filterByCategory(modes, category)
            }
            
            if jsonOutput {
                return outputJSON(modes)
            }
            
            // Pretty table output
            printModesTable(modes)
            return nil
        },
    }
}
```

## Human Output Format
```
REASONING MODES (25 available)

FORMAL (3 modes)
  deductive        Classical logical inference, truth-preserving
  constraint       SAT/SMT/CSP satisfiability reasoning
  type-theoretic   Type-based invariant enforcement

AMPLIATIVE (5 modes)
  abductive        Inference to the best explanation
  analogical       Structure mapping from known domains
  ...

Use 'ntm ensemble modes show <id>' for details
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] List shows all modes with short descriptions
- [ ] Category filter works
- [ ] Show displays full mode details
- [ ] JSON output includes all fields
- [ ] Colors/icons for visual appeal","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:39:19.082388493Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T09:30:52.496456258Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T09:30:52.496410091Z","close_reason":"Merged into bd-28o9m","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-t5ap9","depends_on_id":"bd-qn9gc","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-t9qp4","title":"Feature: Robot Bulk Assign Command (--robot-bulk-assign)","description":"## Overview

Add `ntm --robot-bulk-assign <session>` command that intelligently allocates
beads to agent panes in a single operation based on bv triage output.

## Background

Current bead allocation workflow is tedious:
1. Run `bv --robot-triage` to get prioritized beads
2. Parse the recommendations manually
3. For each pane, decide which bead to assign
4. Construct and send individual prompts to each pane
5. Track the allocation mentally

This should be one command that does it all.

## Proposed Interface

```bash
# Auto-fetch bv triage and allocate
ntm --robot-bulk-assign myproject --from-bv

# Provide explicit allocation
ntm --robot-bulk-assign myproject --allocation='{"2":"bd-92e1","3":"bd-55pm"}'

# Dry run - show plan without executing
ntm --robot-bulk-assign myproject --from-bv --dry-run
```

## Proposed Output

```json
{
  "session": "myproject",
  "strategy": "impact",
  "timestamp": "2026-01-19T23:16:00Z",
  "assignments": [
    {
      "pane": 2,
      "bead": "bd-92e1",
      "bead_title": "Add assignment tracking",
      "reason": "highest_unblocks (9 items)",
      "agent_type": "claude",
      "status": "assigned",
      "prompt_sent": true
    },
    {
      "pane": 3,
      "bead": "bd-55pm",
      "bead_title": "Direct bv integration",
      "reason": "high_unblocks (7 items)",
      "agent_type": "claude",
      "status": "assigned",
      "prompt_sent": true
    },
    // ... more assignments
  ],
  "summary": {
    "total_panes": 15,
    "assigned": 15,
    "skipped": 0,
    "failed": 0
  },
  "unassigned_beads": ["bd-xyz"],  // More beads than panes
  "unassigned_panes": []           // More panes than beads (if any)
}
```

## Allocation Strategies

When using `--from-bv`, use these strategies:

| Strategy | Logic |
|----------|-------|
| impact (default) | Highest `unblocks_count` first |
| ready | Only `ready` status beads, by priority |
| stale | `in_progress` with no recent activity first |
| balanced | Mix of blockers, ready, and stale |

## Flags

- `--from-bv`: Fetch bv triage and auto-allocate
- `--strategy=<S>`: Allocation strategy (impact, ready, stale, balanced)
- `--allocation=<JSON>`: Explicit pane-to-bead mapping
- `--dry-run`: Show plan without executing
- `--skip-panes=<N,N>`: Skip specific panes (e.g., leave them on current work)
- `--prompt-template=<FILE>`: Custom prompt template for assignments

## Integration with BV

This command should:
1. Call `bv --robot-triage` internally (or accept piped input)
2. Parse recommendations
3. Apply allocation strategy
4. Consider existing ntm assign infrastructure (bd-9tbv)

## Prompt Template

Default template for bead assignment:
```
Read AGENTS.md, register with Agent Mail. Work on: {bead_id} - {bead_title}.
Use br show {bead_id} for details. Mark in_progress when starting. Use ultrathink.
```

## Why This Matters

Reduces "spawn and allocate" from a 15-minute manual process to a 5-second command.
Intelligent allocation based on graph topology ensures high-impact work gets done first.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:16:59.960447843Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T02:02:16.011654241Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T02:02:16.011607202Z","close_reason":"Implemented --robot-bulk-assign with strategies, allocation, and dry-run support","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-t9qp4","depends_on_id":"bd-258a1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
+{"id":"bd-u4cfd","title":"Feature: Redaction middleware for REST/WS (requests, responses, logs)","description":"## Summary
As NTM’s REST/WS/web platform expands, ensure sensitive strings are not accidentally echoed back via API responses, websocket events, or server logs.

## Scope
- Apply redaction engine to:
  - request bodies where messages/prompts are accepted
  - response bodies/events where prompts/outputs may be returned
  - server-side logs that may include user text

## Design Notes
- Redaction should be opt-in configurable but recommended by default for remote binds.
- Preserve debuggability: include redaction summary counts/categories.

## Dependencies
- Reuse the unified redaction engine (bd-3cgwv feature).

## Acceptance Criteria
- No raw synthetic secret strings appear in REST/WS payloads when redaction enabled.
- Unit tests for middleware.
- Integration tests for at least one REST endpoint and one WS event.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:34:06.638063553Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-01T03:34:06.647444254Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-u4cfd","depends_on_id":"bd-2ku3r","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:34:06.647413867Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf"}]}
 {"id":"bd-u91ot","title":"Feature: Findings Deduplication UI","description":"## Summary
UI for reviewing and managing duplicate/similar findings across modes before synthesis.

## Rationale
Multiple modes often surface the same issue. Deduplication improves synthesis quality while preserving provenance.

## Implementation
- Use deterministic similarity + clustering from the dedupe engine.
- Dashboard shows clusters with:
  - Primary finding + related findings
  - Source modes + provenance
  - Merge/split controls
- CLI: `ntm ensemble dedupe --interactive` and `--auto --threshold`.

## Logging
- Log cluster sizes, merge actions, and threshold used.

## Tests
- Unit: clustering + merge/split operations
- Integration: UI renders clusters
- E2E: CLI interactive + auto dedupe flows

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Similar findings detected and grouped deterministically
- [ ] Dashboard cluster UI works
- [ ] Merge/split operations preserve provenance
- [ ] CLI interactive mode functional
- [ ] Threshold is configurable and logged

## Dependencies
- OutputSchema (bd-1wnxl)
- Provenance tracking (bd-364w3)
- Redundancy normalization (bd-254do)
- Dashboard panel (bd-121pa)","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T21:07:41.702367333Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T22:00:41.912515679Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-u91ot","depends_on_id":"bd-121pa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-u91ot","depends_on_id":"bd-1h4ox","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-u91ot","depends_on_id":"bd-1wnxl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-u91ot","depends_on_id":"bd-254do","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-u91ot","depends_on_id":"bd-364w3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ubhl","title":"E2E Tests: ntm init workflow with detailed logging","description":"E2E Tests for ntm init workflow with detailed logging.

## Test Scenarios

1. Fresh project initialization
   - Create temp directory
   - Run ntm init
   - Verify all directories created
   - Verify config files valid
   - Log each step with timestamps

2. Re-initialization with --force
   - Init a project
   - Modify config
   - Re-init with --force
   - Verify clean state restored

3. Init with existing partial structure
   - Create partial .ntm directory
   - Run ntm init
   - Verify missing pieces filled in
   - Existing files preserved

4. Integration with flywheel tools
   - Run ntm init
   - Verify Agent Mail can connect
   - Verify CM can initialize
   - Verify bv can read project

## Detailed Logging Requirements
```
[E2E] Step N/TOTAL: DESCRIPTION
[E2E] Creating directory: PATH
[E2E] Writing config: PATH (N bytes)
[E2E] PASS: TEST_NAME in DURATION
[E2E] FAIL: TEST_NAME - expected X, got Y
```

## Test Artifact Collection
- Capture all stdout/stderr
- Save config files on failure
- Create test report JSON with pass/fail/timing

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 4 test scenarios implemented
2. Fresh init creates all expected directories
3. --force restores clean state
4. Partial init fills missing pieces
5. Flywheel integration verified
6. Detailed step-by-step logging
7. Test report JSON generated on completion
8. Exit code 0 on all pass, non-zero on any failure","acceptance_criteria":"1. E2E tests verify ntm init workflow

5. Detailed logging throughout

 {"id":"bd-v5fqf","title":"Task: Implement context pack caching layer","description":"Cache context packs to reduce redundant generation.

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/context_cache.go
type ContextCache struct {
    CacheDir string  // ~/.cache/ntm/context-packs/
    TTL      time.Duration
}

func NewContextCache() *ContextCache
func (c *ContextCache) Get(projectRoot string) (*ContextPack, bool)
func (c *ContextCache) Set(projectRoot string, pack *ContextPack) error
func (c *ContextCache) Invalidate(projectRoot string) error
func (c *ContextCache) InvalidateAll() error

// Cache key computation
func (c *ContextCache) computeKey(root string) string
func (c *ContextCache) isStale(pack *ContextPack) bool
```

## Invalidation Triggers
- Code changes (git status dirty)
- New commits since generation
- TTL expiry (default 1 hour)
- User explicit request (--no-cache)

## Storage Format
```
~/.cache/ntm/context-packs/
  {project-hash}.json  # Serialized ContextPack
  {project-hash}.meta  # Metadata (generated_at, git_sha)
```

 {"id":"bd-vcmnv","title":"EPIC: CLI Help Simplification (minimal/full + optional suggestions)","description":"## Goal
Reduce cognitive overload without gating functionality.

## Decision
- Replace tiered/proficiency gating with a **simple help verbosity switch**.
- Keep **contextual suggestions** as an optional, non-intrusive aid.

## Scope
- `ntm help --minimal` and `ntm help --full` (or equivalent)
- Optional suggestions module (disable-able via config)
- No changes to command availability

## Non-Goals
- No tiers, promotions, or usage tracking.

## Success Criteria
- Minimal help is concise and stable
- Full help remains complete
- Suggestions are helpful but unobtrusive","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

 {"id":"bd-ve9hj","title":"Global spawn scheduler with paced pane/agent creation","description":"Implement a global spawn scheduler that serializes and paces all pane + agent creation (tmux new-session/split + agent process launch). Provide queueing, max concurrent spawns, and a rate limiter (token/leaky bucket with optional burst), preserving request ordering and fairness across sessions without removing any existing behaviors. Surface spawn progress/ETA in TUI/robot outputs so users can see queued work. Integrate hooks for guardrails/backoff. Tests: comprehensive unit tests for limiter math, queue fairness/ordering, starvation prevention, burst handling, and scheduler pause/resume; e2e script that spawns N panes and validates pacing via timestamps, absence of EAGAIN, and detailed spawn logs.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-24T03:19:38.539134018Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-24T09:44:00.476913992Z","closed_at":"2026-01-24T09:44:00.476356702Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["resource-limits","stability"]}
 {"id":"bd-vmwdj","title":"Task: OpenAPI 3.1 generation + Swagger UI","description":"## Scope\nGenerate OpenAPI 3.1 spec from Command Kernel metadata and serve Swagger UI at /docs.\n\n## Implementation\n- OpenAPI generator reads kernel registry (commands, schemas, examples).\n- Emit checked‑in openapi.json for CI diffing.\n- Serve /docs (Swagger UI) + /api/v1/openapi.json.\n\n## Logging\n- Log spec generation with command count and schema count.\n\n## Tests\n- Unit: generator outputs deterministic JSON (stable ordering).\n- Contract: oasdiff (or equivalent Go diff) in CI against checked‑in spec.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- Swagger UI available at /docs.\n- Spec generation is deterministic and CI‑enforced.\n","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:49:30.800864099Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T19:55:14.201716406Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T19:55:14.201583616Z","close_reason":"Implemented OpenAPI 3.1 generator reading from kernel registry.
